title: Add Store cart client and browser reference adapter
verify: Client uses the installed Medusa Store create/retrieve/add/update/delete cart routes with a publishable key.
Reference adapter reads, writes, versions, and clears only the eshop.cart.v1 envelope.
Malformed, unsupported-version, empty, and stale-reference handling cannot reconstruct cart truth from browser data.
Line quantity updates are absolute and transport errors map to stable application states.

verify.closure_decision: Manual closure explicitly requested by the user.
verify.summary: Manual T2 functional verification passed. The required packet was ready and hash-matched before the verdict write; installed Medusa 2.16 handlers matched the specified Store routes; focused and full storefront tests proved publishable-key transport, absolute quantity updates, stable errors, exact reference-only storage, malformed/stale reference handling, and no authoritative browser cart payload; storefront typecheck, strict doctor, and Memory Bank lint passed. GENERAL closed the task after explicit user instruction.

evidence_required: Assertions proving browser storage contains no cart payload, auth token, total, or availability data.
constraints: Reuse Medusa Store cart REST routes rather than a duplicate CRUD facade.
Persist only the versioned opaque cart reference.
invariants: Medusa Cart Module/PostgreSQL owns items, quantities, totals, and ownership.
SKU text is not a cart line identity.
purpose: Provide the narrow browser-to-Medusa boundary for guest cart persistence before UI integration.
success_outcome: Storefront code can operate a Medusa cart and restore it from a safe versioned opaque reference.
anti_goals: Do not add cart UI in this task.
Do not implement authenticated merge or OAuth.
Do not cache authoritative cart payloads in local storage.

stop_conditions: Installed Medusa Store route behavior contradicts the API contract.
The adapter would need to persist cart contents or auth credentials.
Verification cannot distinguish browser reference from backend cart truth.
